definitions.technical: A proposed requirement that a stablecoin issuer notify both the OCC and the CFTC within 24 hours when a reserve custodian enters FDIC receivership or an equivalent proceeding.
definitions.lay: A proposed rule that would require stablecoin issuers to alert both the OCC and the CFTC within one day whenever a bank holding their reserves fails. This ensures both agencies know immediately and can coordinate their response.
definitions.parenthetical: (a proposed 24 hour dual-agency notification requirement triggered by reserve custodian failure)
definitions.legal_significance: The comment letter proposes cross-notification as a practical mechanism to address the jurisdictional ambiguity created by GENIUS Act Section 17(f). Without it, the CFTC may not learn of a stress event until the commodity exclusion has already lapsed.
